skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
stem: A researcher conducted a study to determine whether a new vocabulary app improves high school students' performance on spelling quizzes. The researcher selected a random sample of 42 students from all of the students at one of the high schools in a large city. The researcher found that students who used the app showed significant improvement in spelling quiz scores. What is the largest group to which the results can be generalized?

answerOptions.content: All high school students in the city

rationale: The random sample was drawn from students at one specific high school, so results generalize to that school's students but no farther. Choice B is too narrow because random sampling supports inference about the broader sampling frame. Choices C and D are too broad because students at only one school were sampled.
